topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox like a physical blow. My stomach clenched, a tight, acidic knot forming beneath my ribs. I clicked it open, my hands trembling slightly as I scanned the text. My meticulously crafted analysis, the one I’d poured weeks into, was there. But the byline? It belonged to a stranger. A name I didn't recognize, a name that didn't belong to me. A slow, hot flush crept up my neck, turning my face a deep crimson. I stood up so abruptly my chair scraped loudly across the floor. I paced the small office, my jaw tight, the air in the room suddenly feeling too thin to breathe. Every word I’d written, every insight I’d uncovered, was being claimed by someone else. I slammed my fist onto the desk, the cheap veneer groaning under the impact. "This is impossible," I whispered, the sound raw and brittle. I started furiously typing, not to correct, but to dismantle, to tear apart the deception piece by agonizing piece.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the words swimming before my eyes. A cold, sharp wave washed over me, instantly followed by a burning intensity that made my vision narrow. It was a calculated theft, a brazen act of plagiarism masquerading as journalism. My breath hitched, sharp and shallow. I felt a sudden, overwhelming need to lash out, to shatter whatever fragile composure I had left. I grabbed my phone, my fingers fumbling for the contact information of the editor, my voice dropping to a dangerous, low register. "Where is this? Who authorized this?" The silence on the other end felt mocking. I paced the length of my cramped apartment, kicking at the worn rug, my knuckles white where I gripped my phone. The injustice of it coiled in my gut, a heavy, suffocating pressure that demanded release. I wanted to scream until my throat tore, to make the whole world acknowledge the blatant theft.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, the color drained from my face, replaced by a furious, simmering heat that radiated outward. I read the attribution twice, then three times, the letters blurring into an infuriating mosaic. My breath hitched, a ragged, uneven sound escaping my lungs. A strange, vibrating energy surged through my limbs, making my hands shake uncontrollably. I dropped the tablet onto the coffee table with a decisive thud that echoed in the quiet room. I stood rigid, my entire body coiled tight, every muscle tense. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent city below, my breathing becoming shallow and rapid. The sheer audacity of it—to steal my voice, my work—ignited a fierce, blinding anger. I turned back, my eyes blazing, and started methodically gathering every piece of evidence I had, my movements sharp, precise, and utterly devoid of restraint.
